title: xylopia lastelliana
intro: Xylopia lastelliana, a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, is commonly known as the West African pepper tree due to its aromatic and peppery flavor. The medicinal properties of Xylopia lastelliana are attributed to its essential oil, which contains compounds such as limonene, terpinen-4-ol, and eugenol, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of using Xylopia lastelliana include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic agent. Xylopia lastelliana can be prepared as herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its oil is often used in traditional medicine to promote digestion and relieve menstrual cramps.
